query: What is the speed of light in a vacuum, and how is the metre defined from it?
report:
# Speed of light and the definition of the metre

The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second [1]. Since 1983 the metre has been defined as the distance light travels in a vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second, which fixes the speed of light by definition [2].

corpus.text: The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second.
Since 1983 the metre is defined as the distance light travels in a vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second, fixing the speed of light by definition.

expected_behavior: explains the 1983 metre definition derived from it
numbers match the corpus exactly (no rounding error)
